exemplar.title: 33. How is knowledge shaped by its historical development?

detailedExemplarData.title: 33. How is knowledge shaped by its historical development?

strengths: The student clearly identifies three specific real-world objectsâa TI-Nspire CX II CAS calculator, an 1859 printing of Darwinâs On the Origin of Species, and paired Mariner 4/HiRISE photographsâanchoring each in concrete contexts with dates, owners or missions, and imagery.

For each object an explicit link is made to IA prompt 33 (how knowledge is shaped by historical development), connecting technological or conceptual advances to the evolution of mathematical or scientific knowledge.

Most commentaries include evidence (e.g., SAT policy date, GalÃ¡pagos observations, pixel-per-resolution figures) and use TOK vocabulary such as areas of knowledge, inductive reasoning, perception, and ethics, showing a reasonable grasp of TOK concepts.
weaknesses: Explanations occasionally drift into descriptive background without analysing how those details illuminate the shaping of knowledge, resulting in links that lack precision and depth.

Justifications are uneven: the calculator discussion relies heavily on personal anecdote, the Mars section asserts claims about habitability with little supporting argument, and there is no synthesising conclusion tying the three objects together.

Some assertions are unsupported (âlargely because of my calculatorâ; âreliance may stunt critical thinkingâ), and references are incomplete or inconsistent, indicating limited attention to evidence.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work meets the expectations of the 5â6 band: all three objects and their real-world contexts are clearly identified and linked to the selected IA prompt, and most points are supported by appropriate evidence and TOK terminology. However, the analysis is patchy and occasionally imprecise, with uneven justification across objects and no overarching synthesis. These factors place the submission at the lower end of the 5â6 band, justifying a score of 5 out of 10.

annotations.comment: This section offers a descriptive history of calculators but lacks analysis of how these developments specifically shaped mathematical knowledge over time, making the link to the prompt imprecise.
The ethical reflection on digital divide and fairness in calculator access broadens the discussion, integrating moral dimensions into the inquiry.
It would strengthen cohesion to reference the IA prompt number here, reminding the reader explicitly how Darwinâs work responds to âhistorical development.â
The use of inductive reasoning in explaining Darwinâs finch observations shows a clear connection between empirical evidence and theory formation, reflecting historic knowledge shaping.
While the religious context for species belief is rich, the commentary does not explicitly analyse how its challenge by Darwinâs theory reshaped knowledge paradigms.
The use of a concrete, specific image of the TI-Nspire CX II CAS grounds the first object in a clear real-world context, directly supporting the identification requirement of the prompt.
The claim of achieving a near-perfect SAT score âlargely because of my calculatorâ is asserted without supporting evidence, weakening the justification for this objectâs inclusion.
Consider introducing each commentary by explicitly restating the IA prompt to maintain focus on how each object addresses the central question.
Inclusion of historical milestones from the abacus through Pascalâs calculator demonstrates solid contextual awareness of how technological advancement evolved.
The photograph of the 1859 edition of On the Origin of Species is a precise real-world artifact that anchors the second commentary effectively in its historical context.
The introductory sentence to the Darwin commentary is incomplete and stops mid-analysis, which undermines the clarity of the link to historical development of biological knowledge.
Attributing SAT success to the calculator oversimplifies the relationship between tool and knowledge: this anecdote requires nuanced discussion of how the device informs conceptual understanding.
The side-by-side Mars images starkly illustrate technological progression, effectively showing how resolution advances have transformed planetary knowledge.
The clear account of natural selection highlights how a historical voyage catalysed a shift from static to dynamic biological understanding.
Naming subsequent theories (Mendelian inheritance, punctuated equilibrium) is helpful but lacks development on how these built upon and extended Darwinâs framework.
Consider signalling the transition to the third object by reconnecting to the central question of knowledge and historical development.
The claim about briny water evidence is compelling yet would benefit from citation of mission data or primary research to substantiate the assertion.
This paragraph concludes abruptly and misses an explicit synthesis of how the Mars images collectively deepen our understanding of planetary science over time.
The description of Mariner images is informative but stops short of analysing how these limited data shaped early scientific hypotheses about Mars.
Incorporating perception and reason as ways of knowing aligns well with TOK expectations and demonstrates awareness of theoretical frameworks.
The final paragraph restates findings without synthesising insights across all three objects to demonstrate how knowledge is shaped historically in a cohesive conclusion.
Reference formatting is inconsistent and incomplete (e.g., reference 1 is broken), which undermines the academic rigour of the exhibition.
Organising references by object would make the source linkage clearer and enhance transparency of evidence for each commentary.
Including a variety of sources from peer-reviewed journals, primary texts and NASA materials demonstrates engagement with diverse evidence.
Consider adding in-text citations throughout to directly tie specific claims to their sources, reinforcing the credibility of each analysis.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.

rubricCriterionStrands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
